п»ї---
title: 'Chapter 2 - Dinosaurs, Magic Lessons, and a Wolf Killer'
date: '2025-02-22T09:01:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: false
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2240407711243564043'
blogger_filename: '/2025/02/dinosaurs-magic-lessons-and-wolf-killer.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'MSB'
---

<div>Markus felt stiff, and he stretched and yawned. As his brain slowly booted, he realized he was naked, covered in some kind of blanket, and he opened his eyes to see the apex of a tent.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Heart racing, he sat up as quickly as he could manage his aching bones, and looked around. He found himself draped in a tartan rug, and the smell of campfire smoke wafted into the tent. He looked through the slit in the tent, and could just make out three suns low in the sky but rising.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Ugh,Гўв‚¬Вќ he said to himself. Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm still in the nightmare.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Nightmare?Гўв‚¬Вќ A deep bellowing voice laughed. Гўв‚¬Е“ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs some way to talk about my camp. I donГўв‚¬в„ўt think it is half bad, considering the needless comforts you humans require.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Janie the cat was gone from his feet, and Markus peeked his head outside of the tent.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Sitting on the far side of the campfire, was a massive humanoid, with green and grey scales, a wide shield-like head, with three horns. His hands were three large fingers and a thumb, which were holding a pot and a ladle.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus froze, unsure how to proceed.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Come. Come, child. You must be hungry after a long sleep.Гўв‚¬Вќ The deep voice of the creature encouraged. Гўв‚¬Е“Janie said you might be wary, but I didnГўв‚¬в„ўt expect this kind skittishness. Come, come. I donГўв‚¬в„ўt bite... RatherГўв‚¬В¦ I do... But I wonГўв‚¬в„ўt bite you.Гўв‚¬Вќ The large triangular head bobbed as he smiled.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Um-Гўв‚¬Е“ Markus started, then held the blanket around him tighter, Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬В¦ umГўв‚¬В¦ I am naked.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Ah, Yes.Гўв‚¬Вќ The gargantuan creature said. Гўв‚¬Е“I forgot, Janie left these for you.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>The creature waved a massive hand and a small bundle, tied with twine, floated into the air and then toward MarkusГўв‚¬в„ўs tent, setting down just inside. The creature nodded, and went back to reading a book that looked far too small for his massive hands.</div><div><br /></div><div>At the top of the bundle, under the twine, was a note with a paw stamped into a rainbow colored wax seal.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div>Dearest Markus,</div><div><br /></div><div>This was not the transition I had hoped for, and I am terribly sorry to do this to you, but I have to leave for a bit.&nbsp; It has come to my attention that there have been some developments that require my direct attention. Apparently, I have been gone far too long. It was worth, you were worth it, but, I must attend to this for a bit.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Worry not, I have left you in the best hands I could find. KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh is a dear friend and a wise sage. If anyone can help you get a real start on this journey, it will be him. Alas, while I am a Watcher and Seer, I have never been a great teacher. A storyteller, yes. Oh my yes, but not a teacher.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Listen to him, watch him, and pay attention to your inner self, it will guide you.</div><div><br /></div><div>I will try to return as soon as I can, but for now, stay with KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh until he delivers you safely to a human settlement.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Your Watcher, Janie the Cat</div><div><br /></div><div>PS: I burned your clothes, they were stained and would stand out in this reality. I know you watch too many movies, but I couldnГўв‚¬в„ўt resist setting you up with this package.</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus untied the twine, and pulled the cloth wrapping apart. Inside, he found a Kilt in the blue-green tartan, a black tunic, a belt with some small bags hanging on it, and a black cloak. Along with this, he found a T-shirt from back home, which sported a stylized hand of the Vulcan greeting.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Finding the other materials surprisingly higher quality and better fit than his Ren Faire gear back home, Markus put himself together and tugged at everything a few times to ensure it was together. He found the bags held a few coins, and a small utility knife was sheathed at his side.</div><div><br /></div><div>The creature, apparently KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh, was still reading and lost in thought as Markus stepped out of the tent and into the light of three suns, cloak draped easily over his shoulders, hood back. The smell of food hit him from the fireplace, just now noticing how hungry he felt.</div><div><br /></div><div>Tentatively, Markus stood and walked across the yard to the fire and sat on the opposing log. Гўв‚¬Е“Is that breakfast?Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus asked hoping it was edible for a human.</div><div><br /></div><div>The massive creature bowed his head slightly, set down his book, and filled a bowl using a ladle. With a dexterity belying his size, he placed a spoon inside the bowl, and reached his hand out. The bowl floated across the fire slowly, and paused in floating in front of Markus, who received it.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh,Гўв‚¬Вќ the creature said, with a guttural sound at the end of the word, as if clearing his throat. It reminded Markus of Klingon, or the German composer Johann Sebastian Bach.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm Markus,Гўв‚¬Вќ he said, raising the spoon to his mouth.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>For a while, neither spoke. KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh allowing Markus to take in the scenery and give an appraising few glances at him.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“SoГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ After a long silence, Markus broke the ice. Гўв‚¬Е“Talking cat, talking wolves, now a talking Triceratops man.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Hey now,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh laughed. Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm not sure what a triceratops is, but IГўв‚¬в„ўm a Threxan. Of the horde of KoГўв‚¬в„ўEnns, keepers of the ancient wisdoms. Please to make your acquaintance young human.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm not exactly young, IГўв‚¬в„ўm 36.Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus offered. Then speaking out-loud but more to himself, Гўв‚¬Е“Great, IГўв‚¬в„ўm talking to a dinosaur man.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Our young-lings donГўв‚¬в„ўt even leave the heard until they are at least 100-years. IГўв‚¬в„ўm still only middle aged, and I am 2,000 years old. So, forgive me, but you are a young one. Though, for humans, I understand you are middle aged yourself. So, from your perspective, I can see how you would not feel so young.Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh let out a slow bellowing laugh.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“When you are quite ready, there is much to do, a big journey ahead of us.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“A journey, where?Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus asked.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Janie left on an urgent duty, but assigned me to escort you to a village of your fellow humans. No wolf packs will bother you with me around. Vile creatures.Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh shook his head at the thought of them.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Wolves?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“All dogs, vile, Just vile. Never trust a dog, even the cute ones, theyГўв‚¬в„ўre always making a mess of things. Cats. They are your allies.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus couldn't argue, he had been a cat person, or, at least, a cat adopting him was the only way he ever decided to keep an animal, one who, apparently can talk, and also have urgent business. So he was feeling less sure that was ever his decision to begin with.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>After Markus was done eating, and set his bowl down, KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh stood and waved his hands in a flowing motion over the entire campsite.</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus took in the sheer size of the creature. He stood at least eight feet tall with a mass easily in the hundreds of pounds, and yet the ground only shook slightly when he walked. He was draped in a long robe with symbols all over it, with a large hammer in his belt, as well as cooking pots and other paraphernalia hanging about.</div><div><br /></div><div>The bowl Markus had set down vanished, the campfire was suddenly cold and smokeless, the tent disappeared behind him. The rocks around the campfire rolled away into an unorganized layout. In less than two minutes, the campsite was missing as though it had never existed.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Take nothing but pictures, leave nothing but footsteps,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus murmured to himself.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“That is a lovely saying,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh said. Гўв‚¬Е“I shall add it to my texts.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Leaving the former campsite, KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh began walking into the woods then looked back to see if Markus was following.</div><div><br /></div><div>Seeing no other choice, Markus stood to follow. Гўв‚¬Е“I wonder if they have burritos?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div>For what Markus guessed was half a day, though with three suns it is hard to tell, they worked their way through forests, up and over rocky hills, through streams, and into a slowly changing landscape of new trees. The original forest had been primarily pine trees, but now various other trees dotted the land as well.</div><div><br /></div><div>Throughout the journey, KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh walked with an agility that belied his size. As they walked, Markus thought about how much Janie the Human would have loved this scenery, and would probably have been twice as brave as he was. Plus, she loved camping, while MarkusГўв‚¬в„ў idea of camping was a hotel without room service, not that heГўв‚¬в„ўd seen a hotel in the last few years as his fortunes dried up.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Every so often, Markus slapped his face again, to make sure he wasnГўв‚¬в„ўt dreaming, or force himself to wake up. No such luck, they were still walking. At least he was wearing his new boots, provided by Janie the Cat, which he found were shockingly still comfortable even after hours of walking. He wondered if they were magic boots too.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Finally, they came to the first sign of civilization, a sturdy stone bridge crossing a wide river.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Excuse me, KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh, what is this place called?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“This place?Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh asked, pointing to the bridge. Гўв‚¬Е“Is Maplewood Crossing.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Across the river, there were maple trees as far as MarkusГўв‚¬в„ў could see, the pine trees stopping at this side of the riverГўв‚¬в„ўs edge.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“No,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus replied. Гўв‚¬Е“I mean, this place, thisГўв‚¬В¦ planet? This reality?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh stopped their trek across the bridge, turned around, and placed an elbow on the bridge railing. They were halfway across the bridge, which may have been at least a mile wide, Markus guessed.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The giant gave him an assessing stare, then spoke.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“When Janie, as you know her, asked me to watch over you,Гўв‚¬Вќ he said. Гўв‚¬Е“I never imagined you would be so poorly educated. You donГўв‚¬в„ўt seem to be much a magician, and youГўв‚¬в„ўve never met a Threxan, and you seem soГўв‚¬В¦&nbsp; helpless, if youГўв‚¬в„ўll excuse the tactlessness. Then again, you do seem like the kind of case she would find fascinating.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“UmГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus said, while fiddling with the old ring on his necklace, which Janie the Cat had left on his naked body. It was his wedding ring, which he now wore for a memorial, and sometimes for stimming when heГўв‚¬в„ўs overwhelmed, which he always was.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus down, resting his aching legs, leaning against one of the rail posts on the other side of the bridge. Гўв‚¬Е“Thanks, I think? ButГўв‚¬В¦ do you have a name for thisГўв‚¬В¦ reality? Do you know what planets are?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“The translation magic may not be working well in this case. Your village must be a long way away.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“You have no idea, compadre, you have no idea.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“You must tell me about it sometime, but later, when I can get my recording crystals set up.Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh sat down for himself against a rail post, somehow managing to bring his legs into something approximating a lotus pose. Гўв‚¬Е“Let me see if I can give an answer that would help. This, planet, as you call it, is one of three inhabitable orbs which rotates our three suns.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Three habitable planets?!Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus exclaimed.</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh smiled and nodded. Гўв‚¬Е“You really donГўв‚¬в„ўt get out much. Yes, three, though, not to humans, per se. You are all so fragile, especially if you are without training in the Manna arts.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўve been sheltered,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus offered. Гўв‚¬Е“Apparently more than I realized.Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus made a note to himself to follow up on what Manna Arts might be, though after the glowing blue light saber stick event, he had his guesses. He unconsciously rubbed his forearm tattoo, which was visible but no longer glowing.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Indeed,Гўв‚¬Вќ The giant man said, as he stretched his arms and yawned. Гўв‚¬Е“WellГўв‚¬В¦ This orb is called by many names, in many cultures, but many of us have come to call is Ha Aretz .Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>As he spoke, the dino-man pulled out a small scone, like the one Janie the Cat had given him yesterday, and threw it at Markus. KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh filled a fresh skin of water from the river before tossing it as well.</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus felt the energy surge through him again, washing away his tiredness. His arm tattoo glowed, briefly as well, before settling back into white and black ink. Markus always imagined he would be brave enough to get a tattoo, though this wasnГўв‚¬в„ўt what he imagined it would look like. He always thought it would be Yoda and Spock standing together. He guessed nobody here would be able to draw that one, having no reference. Although, he might get them to replicate his shirt, maybe.</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh, having finished his scone in one great bite, continuedГўв‚¬В¦ Гўв‚¬Е“There is also Oceana, which you would find inhospitable, and Zora, which you would find less hospitable. My people cannot visit Oceana, due to not breathing under water, and there are no visitors facilities. However, I have been to Zora a few times. It is primarily a place of fire and lava, which is why Dragonites and other fire beings call it their home.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“DragonBorn,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus sat up straighter. Гўв‚¬Е“There are DragonBorn here?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Dragonites, but yes. So, you have at least heard of them,Гўв‚¬Вќ the dino-man said. Гўв‚¬Е“I suppose that makes sense, they are flashier, their legends and stories travel farther.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Am I likely to meet one?Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus asked. Гўв‚¬Е“Are theyГўв‚¬В¦ friendly?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Friendly enough, I suppose.Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh paused to consider the question. Гўв‚¬Е“They are a tough race, but generally not harmful if not provoked. They can be a bit arrogant though, given they are an apex species.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Fascinating,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus mused. He finished his lembas bread, which is how he now was thinking of it. Гўв‚¬Е“Whatever bakery you got this from is better than where Janie shops.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh laughed and offered more, Markus shook his head no, Гўв‚¬Е“IГўв‚¬в„ўm full, but thank you.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>And the dino-man took another in one bite. Гўв‚¬Е“You are a polite young human.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The large man stood, which Markus took as clue that the rest was over.</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh said, Гўв‚¬Е“As I understand it, Zora used to be much like Ha Aretz, but that may have been before it began orbiting closer to the third sun. Still, as long as you stay in the visitors facility, it is a breathtaking set of views. You might like it, maybe, but it can feel cramped after a while being stuck inside all the timeГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh drifted off with a far-off gaze for a moment. He then sighed a heavy sigh, and nodded to start walking again.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The dino-man hummed a soft tune and Markus trailed behind. He thought about his apartment, and the take-out containers that were piled by his trash can, and wondered how long it would be before someone checked on him. Whether he was really in another reality, or he was lying unconscious on the kitchen floor and Janie the Cat was waiting for him to wake up and feed her, either way, someone might eventually come looking, right? Then again, with his wife gone, no job, no royalties from his book, and his family in another state, it could be months. He hoped he didnГўв‚¬в„ўt get an infestation because of those take-out containers. He might have to start a scone business if he woke up, or, maybe heГўв‚¬в„ўd go work for the taco truck. At least then heГўв‚¬в„ўd get income, and discount burritos.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Whatever this was, he was starting to find it a welcome break from his normal life. No bills, no rent at the moment, just walking through the most beautiful place he had ever seen, with magic scones to keep him full of energy. Still, it had been a long time since heГўв‚¬в„ўd stayed at the MGM Grand or Marriott Desert Springs on a book signing tour or work conference. He missed room service and spa treatments.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“I wonder if they have golf hereГўв‚¬В¦Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus wondered barely audibly.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“What is golf?Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh asked.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Oh,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus said. Гўв‚¬Е“You have excellent hearing, I barely said that out loud.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“My hearing is wider than your human ears can perceive,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh said matter-of-factly.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Well, anyway, golf is a game where you walk around outside on grass that is mowed very short, and you use metal sticks to hit a small ball into a hole.Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus thought that was a sufficient, if crude, explanation.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“I canГўв‚¬в„ўt say I am aware of such a game,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh said. Гўв‚¬Е“But you humans do have different habits than we do, and your games are often not possible for us, due to our size difference.Гўв‚¬Вќ Dino-man shrugged and went back to humming his tune.</div><div><br /></div><div>After the river crossing, they left the dense forests behind. The forest was getting thinner and trees more diversified, fewer pines, more deciduous trees with large three pointed leaves. Markus counted this a great relief, as the pine trees had been hard to navigate.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The new wildlife was fascinating too; birds with long rainbow feathers that glowed brightly against the now overcast sky and perched in the tops of the maples. A family of tiny tree frogs with bulging eyes and purple skin greeted them from a nearby oak. Even then, walking and walking and walking was monotonous for a man whoГўв‚¬в„ўd lived his life behind a keyboard syncing his lifeГўв‚¬в„ўs work to GitHub repos and Excel sheets.</div><div>ГЇВїВј</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“WeГўв‚¬в„ўve been walking for hours,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus griped. Гўв‚¬Е“I enjoy a stroll through the forest as much as the next nerd, but this is exhausting.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Shall I carry you little one?Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh raised his scale encrusted eye brow, with what might be a smirk.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“No,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus sulked. Гўв‚¬Е“I justГўв‚¬В¦ Are we getting close?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“It should be just over the next few hills.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Well, then,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus found himself rubbing his forearm tattoo again. Гўв‚¬Е“Can you tell me about this?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Lifting the inside of his forearm to show KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh his tattoo. The dino-man paused, briefly, and bent over to see it more closely.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“You donГўв‚¬в„ўt know about your own soul mark?Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh, a little incredulous.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Soul mark?Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus shrugged. Гўв‚¬Е“Almost as soon as Janie the Cat brought me here, to this realityГўв‚¬В¦ orbГўв‚¬В¦ whateverГўв‚¬В¦ we got attacked by three white wolves, and she told me to pick up a stick to defend myself, which I thought was a bit underwhelming as a defensive item.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh eyed the tattoo another moment, and then started walking again, this time side by side in the open rolling hills.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Interesting,Гўв‚¬Вќ is all the dino-man offered.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Well, the stick started glowing, which reminded me a weapon from stories in my home town. Then the big wolf attacked me and I hit it with the glowing stick, and it died. The other two wolves left, and then this justГўв‚¬В¦ kind of appeared? I guess. On my arm.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Does nobody in your village have soul marks?Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh asked with what sounded like genuine curiosity. Гўв‚¬Е“And this is your first soul mark?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“I have never heard the term,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus said, and shrugged.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Hm,Гўв‚¬Вќ the dino-man pondered this for a bit. Гўв‚¬Е“Where to start. Green as a hatchling this one. I can see why Janie said you needed an escort to your kind.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Wolf Killer,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh said. Гўв‚¬Е“Well, I suppose that makes sense, given your history, and your story. I shall be interested to watch your progress little one.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“What makes you say that?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“A soul mark,Гўв‚¬Вќ the dino-man started. Гўв‚¬Е“They are common among the mages, but usually those of higher class and skill than you seem to have. Most people only achieve theirs when they have faced great hardships. Not the inconveniences of life, mind you, but real hardships.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“That does sound like me,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus replied, not wanting to discuss her right now.</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Even then,Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh continued without prying. Гўв‚¬Е“It is unusual for someone not trained in the Manna Arts to achieve a soul mark of this kind. You must have some innate access to your Manna.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus found himself rubbing the tattoo as he listened. Гўв‚¬Е“Wait, what is Manna?Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh gave a sideways glance at him, but then shook his head and answered. Гўв‚¬Е“Manna is the source of all that is. It is life. It is energy. It is essence. It is matter. There is Manna in everything, but living beings hold it in more concentrated forms. The Manna Arts are how we train to pull the energy from Manna, and shape it according to our needs.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Is that dangerous?Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus asked.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Yes. No. Is fire dangerous?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus pondered a moment, then replied, Гўв‚¬Е“I see.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Fire is simply a lower form of Manna release. The cut wood in our fire pit is converted to energy, which is released as fire.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“That tracks with the education I do have, from myГўв‚¬В¦ village.Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus said. Гўв‚¬Е“We have a technology that takes matter and splits it into energy, powering our homes.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Good, you arenГўв‚¬в„ўt a complete illiterate.Гўв‚¬Вќ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh teased. Гўв‚¬Е“Those who have trained in the Manna Arts generally fall into two types, Generalists and Specialists. As they train, they sometimes face challenges that go beyond the simple use of Manna Arts, they face a situation that changes them on the inside, their soul. That marks them, in a way that forever impacts their use of the Manna Arts.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus sat with that explanation for a few strides. Гўв‚¬Е“I do feel different.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh turned to Markus, stopped walking, and grabbed him by both shoulders to look him in the eye for a moment, searchingly. Гўв‚¬Е“With a soul markГўв‚¬В¦ you cannot have been unchanged.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>After a pause, still searching MarkusГўв‚¬в„ў eyes in a way that made him feel both naked and yet seen, laid bear but accepted, Dino-man finally nodded, more to himself than Markus. Гўв‚¬Е“Hm,Гўв‚¬Вќ he said. Then began walking again.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“So, Manna Cakes, a bit on the nose for a name, must be some form of recharge fuel? Is that why I feel so much stronger after eating one?Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Indeed,Гўв‚¬Вќ Nodding affirmative. KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh continued, Гўв‚¬Е“Energy is matter is energy is matter. If you learn nothing else about Manna from me, learn that. So many Manna Artists forget that basic, and then fail to grasp why something isnГўв‚¬в„ўt working as intended. You cannot release manna without fundamentally changing the thing that releases it. The soul marks-Гўв‚¬Е“ KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh cut off abruptly, still walking, but more cautiously.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Motioning with his palm down to the ground, he warned Markus, Гўв‚¬Е“Stay down, stay back.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus watched the giant glide silently forward, out of the Maplewood into a clearing, peeking over a hill.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Curiosity killed the cat,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus whispered to himself, but couldnГўв‚¬в„ўt resist peaking over the hill as well.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Smoke rose in the distance from buildings clustered together in what must have been a village. Shouting was barely audible from this distance. People were running either from or toward a group of black robed people holding weapons and torches of red glowing flames.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh gave Markus a stern look, motioned for him to stay down, and stood to his full stature atop the hill. As he threw his robe to the air and it disappeared, then he flexed his massive muscles and stretched his arms, making a motion that reminded Markus of Thai Chi or Qigong.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Then he opened his mouth and let out a roar that shook the ground around them. Sigils embedded in each of his scales began to glow and pulse in various colors. Markus hadnГўв‚¬в„ўt noticed them before, against the previously dark grey scales.</div><div><br /></div><div>In the distance, the marauders turned their attention toward the hulking giant, and a group of them broke off, heading his way. As they got closer, Markus could make out swords, axes, maces, staves, and other weapons of various kinds, all showing signs of magical enhancement.</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh let them come his way for a moment, then with blinding speed ran toward them, with an agility and speed Markus did not think possible for any living being. As the giant closed the gap halfway to them, he turned completely invisible.</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus gasped, but put his hand over his mouth to stop the sound.</div><div><br /></div><div>The attackers paused at this development, and created a defensive grouping. The scene was still for a few moments. Then figures from the group began flying away from the formation as if they had been struck by a giantГўв‚¬В¦. Hammer.</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus had wondered, Гўв‚¬Е“I guess thatГўв‚¬в„ўs what that hammer is for...Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Just then, his own arm began to feel warm, and his tattoo started to glow and pulse.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Behind him, wrestling in the bushes.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Standing, looking around him, he found no free sticks this time.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Three wolves, the same three from yesterday came out, with two more behind. Without KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh or Janie, he felt defenseless.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He started to reach to his belt for the knife, but it was far too small to be helpful here. Having nothing else to go on, he picked up a nearby stone. He spoke to it, as before, Гўв‚¬Е“Defend me.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>The stone began to glow and pulse in his hand. Hoping it would do the trick again. He tossed the rock and it landed near one of the wolves, exploding on impact. Гўв‚¬Е“ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs something,Гўв‚¬Вќ Markus said to himself.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The white wolves, undeterred, spread apart to avoid a cluster.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus quickly stuffed the front of his shirt with more rocks, like an uncoordinated Kangaroo. From his new makeshift pouch, he picked a rock and held it up. It glowed, he threw, he missed, it exploded on hitting the ground, a wolf laughed and brushed the dust off.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He threw another at the closest wolf, and it struck the wolf in the face, exploding on impact, killing it instantly.</div><div><br /></div><div>He threw another rock, it missed.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He threw another rock and it bounced off the ground, exploding, but throwing shards of rock into the wolfГўв‚¬в„ўs chest, and it fell. It tried to stand again, but one leg hung useless. It limped on three legs back behind the others.</div><div><br /></div><div>The remaining three wolves circled him.</div><div><br /></div><div>One lunged, jaws open. Markus, holding onto the last three rocks, hit the beast with one of them and it made an impact that was beyond anything he expected. The animal went flying off as though struck by something much bigger, and landed a distance from them.</div><div><br /></div><div>This left two wolves with all their limbs, and one severely injured. They lost their confidence without the pack.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>They took off running.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He hadnГўв‚¬в„ўt noticed, but during the fight, as he kept spinning to see all his potential attackers, he had been wandering closer to the village.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh was nowhere in sight, but a trail of bodies in lay in awkward positions leading all the way to town.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>A young human girl, no older than 10 by MarkusГўв‚¬в„ў guess, came running and screaming from the village, chased by a white wolf. This one was twice the size of any that Markus had faced yet.</div><div><br /></div><div>Armed with his last two rocks and adrenaline energy, Markus went running toward the girl.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Before he could get there, the wolf caught up to her and grabbed her leg, knocking her down.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Mid-stride, Markus threw one of the rocks and it made impact on the side of the creatureГўв‚¬в„ўs shoulder, knocking it down and away from her.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus arrived moments later, and kicked the creature in the belly. It rolled, got up, and growled; a gaping hole where one shoulder should be. It limped on three legs, but unafraid, unlike the one from earlier. The wolf growled, showing its teeth, and lunged at Markus.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He raised his hands to block the attack but dropped the rock, and they both went down.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>After some struggle, one arm out against the wolfГўв‚¬в„ўs jaws, keeping them from biting his face, his other hand felt the rock and he grabbed it. Out of the corner of his eye, he saw the rock glowed brightly and he shoved it into the creaturesГўв‚¬в„ў mouth, then punched its jaw closed. The head exploded on impact spraying him, the girl, the ground with blood. Luckily, this time, he and the girl missed most of it.</div><div><br /></div><div>He got up, a bit dazed, but less grossed out than his first fight yesterday, probably because of the adrenaline pumping through him on overdrive. The blood fell off his clothes, rather than staining them this time.</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus felt a surge of energy flowing through him, like an adrenaline rush but on a level he had never experienced. Then his arm got hot, and the tattoo glowed even brighter, swirls of decorative script wrapped the phrase, Гўв‚¬Е“Wolf Killer.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Breathing heavily, he checked on the girl. She had a bite mark, but it seemed superficial. Still, she couldnГўв‚¬в„ўt put weight on the leg, so he picked her up.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>At this point, in the distance, KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh stood surrounded by a group of people he assumed to be villagers, by the way they were crowding and shaking his hand, no sign of further struggle.</div><div><br /></div><div>As Markus made his way to them, he told the girl to close her eyes as they walked through a trail of dead bodies left in KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkhГўв‚¬в„ўs wake.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Now more intrigued than grossed out, Markus observed the dead people were a rag tag mix of reptilians, somewhat reminiscent of the bearded dragon his friend Jeff had growing up, and humans with pale skin and no hair, all with a The White Wolf branded on their foreheads.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The young girlГўв‚¬в„ўs parents came running to her, and the father grabbed her. The mother worried over her looking at her daughterГўв‚¬в„ўs injuries. Гўв‚¬Е“ItГўв‚¬в„ўs superficial,Гўв‚¬Вќ she said. Waving her hands and lips silently moving, a wash of yellow light emanated from the womanГўв‚¬в„ўs chest to an amulet around her neck, then into her hands, then thorough her hands to her daughterГўв‚¬в„ўs leg.</div><div><br /></div><div>The young girl looked down, wiggled her leg, and smiled. She hopped down from her fatherГўв‚¬в„ўs arms and ran to Markus to give him a hug around his leg. Гўв‚¬Е“Thank you!Гўв‚¬Вќ She said, before jumping back into her fatherГўв‚¬в„ўs arms and hugging him around the neck.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The woman then noticed MarkusГўв‚¬в„ў shoulder. Гўв‚¬Е“That on the other hand,Гўв‚¬Вќ She said. Гўв‚¬Е“Will take some doing. Come, letГўв‚¬в„ўs get that fixed.Гўв‚¬Вќ&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus looked to KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh, who was still too busy to notice, and headed away with the magic mom-nurse-lady.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div>Ten minutes later, Markus found himself in a local inn dining room, turned triage center, having a gash on his shoulder attended to, which he hadnГўв‚¬в„ўt originally noticed until the little girlГўв‚¬в„ўs mother had pointed it out. He was feeling it now that the adrenaline started wearing off.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The young girlГўв‚¬в„ўs mother was sewing a deep gash in his shoulder back together with hand waving and yellow light, which was apparently her specialty. But it was a deep wound and if he didnГўв‚¬в„ўt hold still heГўв‚¬в„ўd have a scar. While it wasnГўв‚¬в„ўt painful, because, magic, he guessed, it still tugged and pulled and it was an odd sensation. Much like the time he had a tooth removed.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>By some miracle nobody could account for, not one member of the village died. The White Wolf Clan claimed to be looking for something, or someone. The reports were mixed. They searched as they stole, broke, and burned down the buildings, but other than a few injuries, some serious in the case of the town guards, not one death.</div><div><br /></div><div>Despite this, there were no survivors of the attacking White Wolf Clan. They had been overpowering the village until KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh arrived plowing through them like a wheat field.</div><div><br /></div><div>After the injured were healed, and the dining room set back up for a meal, the town pitched in together to host a celebration. That felt odd, given that they had just had their homes destroyed, so he asked about the reaction.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“Homes we can rebuild,Гўв‚¬Вќ the mom told him. Гўв‚¬Е“Lives are whatГўв‚¬в„ўs important. We celebrate life today. Life won.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus did not receive as much attention from the village as a whole, as KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh did all the saving and other matters had their attention. However, he found himself the sole focus of the girl and her parents. Their gratitude in saving their daughter from the wolf chasing her down was unmatched. They turned out to be the owners of the inn, and offered Markus a free room for as long as he would like, no charge.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus offered to help the town in any way possible, now that his wound was stitched, but when he admitted no specific magical abilities other than to make sticks and rocks glow, which he himself still didnГўв‚¬в„ўt understand, the inn keepers gave each other a worried glance and then gave Markus a pitiful one.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Гўв‚¬Е“WeГўв‚¬в„ўve got it from here,Гўв‚¬Вќ she assured him. Гўв‚¬Е“You rest.Гўв‚¬Вќ</div><div><br /></div><div>Markus walked outside to see the fires were out as various forms of wind, ice, and water were employed by magical hands.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>The bodies of the dead attackers had been gathered into a single location, and burned away from the city.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>Various craftsmen were working on making repairs to the less damaged buildings.</div><div><br /></div><div>Back in his room, laying on a bed that was significantly more comfortable than his back back home, Markus watched the sky and forests through his large double window.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>On the horizon, the second of three suns were cresting the forest canopy, and the moon was a purple-blue hue as its pink rings became visible over the other horizon. Markus was still so unaccustomed to this realm, he had no bearing of which direction they were rising from and setting to, and his cell phone, battery dead, still wouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt have helped on another planet. He was pretty sure they didnГўв‚¬в„ўt have Google Maps here.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>While his day hadnГўв‚¬в„ўt gone according to anything resembling any expectation of normal, and he was faced with more danger, he was grateful, at least, to have a 1% better understanding of his surroundings than he had yesterday, and two wolf wins gave him just a hairГўв‚¬в„ўs breadth more confidence that he might not die, and now he had room and board for the moment.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>StillГўв‚¬В¦ given that there is no US-Dollar, and magic was a thing, he imagined currency worked differently here.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>So he decided to get some help understanding the lay of the land so he could fend for himself in the days to come.&nbsp;</div><div><br /></div><div>He wondered what KoГўв‚¬в„ўAkh might be doing, and where Janie the Cat had run off to. He wondered what Janie the Human would say if she could see this with him. He wondered what substance makes pink rings on a moon. He wondered if he would wake up from this hallucination tonight. As he drifted off to sleep, he wondered if the inn keeper knew how to make a breakfast burrito.</div><div><br /></div><div>***</div><div><br /></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhkQ3C_-vTGta9Cuh2hcpngl277VLKpHvi4Z9TmJSE8uPao-aZmXWhwft5vk1qQdK4GrOy77hgDLOV5geQ9y-r06t6nVxOv4X-de-oG7UHpwECsJoBfbGi2qbIYEns460tYXGxE5mZeSydZjkx3rONLAamDEF2L1EiOa_F6MVHBN9DwfY5-3OFJjO6YA6o/s1557/MSB_Cover_Placeholder.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="1557" data-original-width="1080" height="320" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhkQ3C_-vTGta9Cuh2hcpngl277VLKpHvi4Z9TmJSE8uPao-aZmXWhwft5vk1qQdK4GrOy77hgDLOV5geQ9y-r06t6nVxOv4X-de-oG7UHpwECsJoBfbGi2qbIYEns460tYXGxE5mZeSydZjkx3rONLAamDEF2L1EiOa_F6MVHBN9DwfY5-3OFJjO6YA6o/s320/MSB_Cover_Placeholder.jpg" width="222" /></a></div><br /><div><br /></div><div><br /></div>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<div id="mc_embed_signup">
<form action="https://darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank">
<div id="mc_embed_signup_scroll">
<label>Subscribe Now - Pursue a No Hiding Lifestyle</label>&nbsp;<input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" required="" type="email" value="" /><br />
<div aria-hidden="true" style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;">
<input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" type="text" value="" /></div>
<div class="clear">
<input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div>
</div>
</form>
</div>
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom: Live Long and Prosper!</span><br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe (DG Wolfe)</span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant @&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/start-here-about_12.html" target="_blank">DarrellWolfe.com</a></span><br />
<br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/" target="_blank">Clifton StrengthsFinder</a>: Intellection, Learner,&nbsp;</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Ideation,</span><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">&nbsp;Achiever, Input</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><a href="https://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" target="_blank">16Personalities</a> (Myers-Briggs Type): INFJ</span><br />
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span>
<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;"><br /></span></div>
